i think this is not needed. Can you recheck please.

As per DB schema, we have two more field - timestamp and version. I think version field will be auto-populated when a record is added but timestamp is something that we get in the input. 

I think Service should accept that domain object ( NetworkData ) as input instead of each field.
Should be a collection as we can have multiple values based on the filtering criteria.

Ack
Do not change docker repository properties, it breaks docker build maven command. See the documentation in README file.

Personally, I used to see benefit to have properties created for pom values that are either:

(1) used at several places in the pom file
or (2) are core fundamental values for the application structure or definition.

I feel that having many properties for values that are used only once and not core fundamental to the application make the pom less straightforward to read and could hide main core properties among many other not so important ones.

Here, I feel that hibernate types version could be inline. For me, it is not as structuring as the application docker image name for example.

This a subjective point of view that can be discussed, I agree ...

Do we have a bug with jacoco configuration ? Are these changes related to this story ?
Same questions for jacoco plugin configuration changes below.

Maybe we could be a bit more precise about the role of this entity. Could it be something like:

Entity to store an anchor configuration or state along with the moment it has been observed.

'lastTimestamp' is suggesting a changing value whose current one is the last one. But as this timestamp is never changing I would have a preference for 'observedTimestamp'.

Or 'observationTimestamp' if we want to make it consistent with 'creationTimestamp'
... for network data.

As retrieving data is not is this story scope, could we either not define this method on the service interface or make it throwing Not Implemented exception to make it clear that it is not there yet.

The default implementation provided here is some how hiding a bit the actual state of the feature.

Is there any reason to change the default 8080 port number ? Also, changing this port number is breaking docker compose configuration.

It is for convenience for the specific situation when developers would like to run both cps and cps core locally, without docker ? 

For docker and kubernetes deployments, It could be worth to a keep services standardized, all using same 8080 port.

Is there any reason to enable lazy loading outside from transactional context ?

For now, there are no related entities of network data that seem to require it. Am I missing something ?

Also, when used, this property can introduce an overhead on db connections, then it should be considered as last option.

Unit test classes should be independent.

This one depends on ApplicationTest to have testcontainers started.
I'm not sure about the need of using GSON library here. What is the difference between the provided string and the PAYLOAD one ?
Indentation formatting ?
It could be interesting to verify that the creation timestamp has been successfully filled in the returned object (and that the observation timestamp is kept unchanged in the returned object).
License is missing.
Same comment as the one for NetworkDataRepositorySpec.
Are the following really setup instanciations that need to be done before each feature method ?
Then clause is not verifying that network data is returned, it is more verifying that network data is asked to be persisted.
Querying is not implemented yet and is not in the scope of the story.

This test class is not an actual test class with assertions. It has only been created to meet the code coverage minimum criteria while there was no code yet for the application. 

Now that we are adding actual code and related test classes, are we now able to delete this ApplicationTest class ?
I just noticed item 3. from the commit message to include spock tests in coverage check. Then I presume this is the explanation for the change.
Ack

Yaa that was done for development convenience .. reverted that to 8080 in the commit
Ack
Ack

Do not use java access modifier in Groovy as per our guidlines, no need for uppercase either
if need use teh type but preffered not to ie.

def myCommonString = 'some example'

use single quotes unless you specifically want to use Groovy-String functionality (substitute variables etc.)
see guidelines for more explanation

setupSpec can be with @Shared (static) data. but in general it is safer to make sure each test has a fresh setup. I only use it for 'expensive' setup and when you are 100% sure that the test won't affect teh (data) you set up.
correct, the test is only verifying that the repository service is called with the correct parameters

not sure why you are verifying properties of networkData (they are part of your tets setup ie you are testing mainly your owntest setup).
You would only do this if the method being tested constructs or manipulated the networkData object.
instead you just need to verify the method is called using the same networkData object itself
agreed, not sure what you are trying to test here.

I'm not really sure about the jacoco configuration change.

From master branch, there is already QueryControllerSpec that is taken into account for code coverage. Anything else missing ?

It seems that the change broke code coverage validation:

  [INFO] --- jacoco-maven-plugin:0.8.5:check (coverage-check) @ cps-temporal ---
  [INFO] Skipping JaCoCo execution due to missing execution data file:/Users/bsakoto/dev/git-repositories/bell/onap/configuration-persistence-service/cps-temporal/**/code-coverage/jacoco-ut.exec

Nice to have comment:

While we are renaming timestamps fields, why not making them completely consistent ?

Either: observedTimestamp and createdTimestamp

Or: observationTimestamp and creationTimestamp
I'm not too familiar with groovy, I presume a kind of NPE is raised if storedData.getCreationTimestamp() is null ... Just to be sure this case is not missed ...

equals() methods from domain are the ones that cause the coverage drop.

Let's keep this package exclusion you added. Then, we'll see if it is enough for sonar that is also checking the conditions coverage percentage (and I'm not sure if the exclusion applies to this one also or not).

We'll come back to the coverage if sonar starts complaining. Anyway, we don't even have enough code to test yet ;)
Ack
as both CreatedTimestamp is non nullable fields in DB, I dont think this test could throw a NPE.
adding lombok.config should take care of that.
excluding coverage would not be acceptable it would easily be forgotten, hopefully Rishi's suggestion fixes it. if not we should (temporaly) lower the coverage threshold instead
acronyms that are common ie. they are almost like words should be camel cased (I think) so this class should eb called 'NetworkDataId'

no need to test it with a populated object, empty object is good enough as you are just verifying it is passed on
